steps.title: Identify the criterion for tall
steps.content: A perennial flower is classified as tall if its height exceeds 100 centimeters. To represent a tall species, an inequality must include at least some possible heights where $h > 100$.

steps.title: Find the range that includes heights greater than 100
steps.content:
Check the maximum value in each inequality:
  • Choice A: $86 < h < 86$ — impossible
  • Choice B: $86 < h < 89$ — all values are less than 100
  • Choice C: $86 < h < 100$ — all values satisfy $h < 100$
  • Choice D: $86 < h < 154$ — includes values greater than 100 (from 100 to 154)
Only choice D's range permits tall plants. That matches choice D — D is correct.
